name: Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive on autopilot

parameters.text: =✅ Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive on autopil — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 6 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Download from Google Drive

### Download from Google Drive

Downloads the file's binary content from Google Drive so the next node can parse it.

Ports: ①→ Parse XLSX Contents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Parse XLSX Contents

Parses the incoming XLSX binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming asset records to a stable schema

Next: Rows Pass Validation?

### Rows Pass Validation?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.assetId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Load Rows into Google Sheets · ②→ Quarantine Bad Rows

### Quarantine Bad Rows

Appends a new row to 'Asset Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Load Rows into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Asset Tracker', matched on `assetId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Repack as XLSX · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Load Rows into Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Repack as XLSX

Serializes items to a XLSX binary file.

Next: Archive Processed File (Google Drive)

### Archive Processed File (Google Drive)

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Stamp Processing Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 50 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Sort Assets Newest First

### Sort Assets Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Guard Non-Empty title

### Guard Non-Empty title

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#media`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#media`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive on autopilot

**Pattern:** Video Production · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual shorts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Download from Google Drive → Parse XLSX Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Rows Pass Validation? → Load Rows into Google Sheets → Repack as XLSX → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to video production without manual effort
- Anyone automating shorts in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Standardizes the process every time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your shorts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
